compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin maintainer, I have a lifecycle policy for suggestion records so the store does not grow unboundedly
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A pruning/archival policy for .suggestions/ that prevents unbounded accumulation
**Why**: Without lifecycle management, hundreds of sessions create thousands of suggestion files, degrading retro-gather.sh performance and eventually exceeding tool timeout ceilings during synthesis
**Scope**:
- IN: Archival or pruning mechanism (e.g., move consumed suggestions to .suggestions/archive/ after retro synthesis, or delete after N days), integration with retro workflow, worktree-cleanup.sh compatibility
- OUT: Complex rotation policies, database-style compaction

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, suggestion records have a defined lifecycle (creation → consumption by retro → archival or deletion)
  ← Satisfies: scenario analysis finding "Suggestion files accumulate unboundedly"
- When this story is complete, retro synthesis performance does not degrade as the suggestion store grows over time
  ← Satisfies: scenario analysis finding "retro performance degrades over hundreds of sessions"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Performance] Consider timestamp-based filtering (only process suggestions since last retro run) as an alternative to deletion
- [Reliability] Archival/pruning must be safe under concurrent writes (flock-aware)

comments.body: SPRINT_DEFERRAL: This story was classified COMPLEX during planning and was not implemented in this sprint. The epic success criteria (SC1-SC6) do not include lifecycle policy — this story addresses the 'unbounded growth' scenario mitigation. Deferring to a future sprint. Related: bug 7fcd-e5bf tracks the tool-use-*.jsonl scanning gap (partial overlap in scope).
comments.author: Test
